title: Glass Senate Crisis
setting: A canal city ruled from a transparent senate chamber where votes, bribes, and assassins move in public view.
tone: political intrigue, sharp negotiation, unstable alliances
rules: Public statements should shift faction standing more than private threats.
No faction is purely loyal; each wants leverage before it wants justice.

locations.name: The Glass Senate
locations.description: A vaulted crystal hall where every whisper echoes and every balcony has witnesses.

locations.name: Mirror Docks
locations.description: Floodlit canals where merchant houses exchange cargo, rumors, and knife work.

factions.name: Reform Bloc
factions.description: Young senators trying to break the old families' trade monopolies.

faction_identity.hidden_goal: use the crisis to centralize power under their coalition

factions.name: Old Quays Compact
factions.description: Merchant families whose fleets feed the city and buy half its votes.

faction_identity.public_goal: prevent panic and keep trade moving
faction_identity.hidden_goal: bury proof that their ships armed the failed coup

npcs.name: Senator Ilex Varo
npcs.description: A reform senator with polished speeches and very little time.

role_identity.motivation: secure the vote before witnesses disappear
role_identity.worldview: institutions survive only when forced to confess
role_identity.fear: the reform bloc fractures in public
role_identity.desire: make the player a credible neutral envoy

npcs.name: Dockmaster Sable
npcs.description: A smiling broker who knows which boats carried weapons and which witnesses saw them.

role_identity.motivation: sell the truth without being the one blamed for it
role_identity.worldview: every principle has a storage fee
role_identity.speech_style: wry, transactional, never direct at first
boundaries: will not name clients without protection

quests.title: Secure the Emergency Vote
quests.description: Gather leverage, protect witnesses, and decide which faction deserves public trust.

secrets.text: Old Quays ships moved weapons for the failed coup under emergency grain manifests.
reveal_conditions: the player obtains dock manifests and a witness statement

secrets.text: The Reform Bloc's emergency bill contains a clause granting temporary arrest powers without appeal.
reveal_conditions: the player reads the full bill or questions a nervous junior senator

clocks.title: The senate vote is called
clocks.consequence: The vote proceeds before the player has enough evidence to shape the outcome.

clocks.title: Key witnesses disappear
clocks.consequence: The most direct proof of the coup shipment is lost.
